items.title: Vanilla RNN hidden states
items.description: h_t = tanh(W_h h_{t−1} + W_x x_t + b) keeps recurrent hidden vectors bounded in (−1, 1) and zero-centered at the origin — the classic use of tanh in sequence models.

items.title: LSTM candidate cell state
items.description: LSTMs apply tanh to the proposed cell content ĉ_t while sigmoid gates control information flow — tanh bounds the candidate; gates scale and blend it.

items.title: GRU reset/update pathways
items.description: GRU variants often use tanh on candidate hidden content combined with sigmoid-controlled gates, mirroring the LSTM tanh-plus-sigmoid pattern.

items.title: Generative model outputs on [−1, 1] data
items.description: When inputs are normalized to [−1, 1] (common in GANs and some autoencoders), a tanh output layer matches the target range without extra scaling.

items.title: Legacy feedforward hidden layers
items.description: Pre-ReLU deep nets used tanh (or sigmoid) hidden activations; still appears in tutorials and small fully connected models where bounded activations are desired.
